summary: Jinneng PP yarn/injection at USD 990/ton FOB; CIF Vietnam equivalent around USD 1,010-1,030/ton
Lotte PP Film cut USD 210 to USD 1,120/ton CIF; PP coating M9600 down USD 80 to around USD 1,420/ton CIF
Domestic NSRP/BSR PP down VND 500-1,000/kg; Hanwha PP copo offered at VND 33,000-34,000/kg
paras: Late last week, one distributor cut its offer for Jinneng (China) PP yarn into the Vietnam market down to 1,025 USD/ton CIF; at the start of this week another distributor was still holding its offer for this product at a high 1,095 USD/ton CIF.

Today, June 30, Jinneng's distributor quoted Jinneng PP yarn/injection at 990 USD/ton on FOB Qingdao terms, and BOPP at 1,000 USD/ton FOB Qingdao, for July 2026 delivery. Since the CIF Vietnam price at this point typically adds 20–30 USD/ton over FOB, Jinneng's PP and BOPP offers would equate to roughly 1,010–1,030 USD/ton CIF.

Even so, this price is not particularly competitive, because Sabic's PP yarn/injection (from the S-Oil plant in Korea) has just been firmed to Vietnamese buyers at 1,020 USD/ton CIF, while Hanwha Korea's PP has been down at 990 USD/ton CIF since last week.

Also in Korean goods, today the distributor of Lotte Chemical quoted new firm selling prices for its August 2026 PP shipment to buyers in Vietnam, trending sharply lower. Specifically, PP coating M9600 was firmed at around 1,420 USD/ton CIF — down 80 USD/ton versus mid-June; the PP Film offer dropped deeply by 210 USD/ton over two weeks to 1,120 USD/ton CIF…

Sibur (Russia) has continued to actively offer PE to Vietnam over the past two weeks but has not offered PP, or has offered very limited volumes to a few of its own customers. The reason may be limited PP availability, or that high prices are uncompetitive versus PP of other origins.

Domestically, early this week some of NSRP's leading distributors offered NSRP's PP 114G at 31,000 VND/kg incl. VAT and incl. delivery in the North, down 500 VND/kg from the offer at the end of last week — and level with the firm selling price at the end of last week.

Notably, BSR's PP T3034 was offered by a leading distributor at 31,400 VND/kg incl. VAT ex-factory warehouse in Quang Ngai — down by as much as 1,000 VND/kg versus the end of last week. Sold into the South this offer adds 600 VND/kg to reach 32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; offered to the North it adds 800 VND/kg to reach 32,200 VND/kg incl. VAT…
Sources say the NSRP and BSR plants are still running at maximum capacity, and PP inventory remains fairly high as exports in the second half of June are slowing compared with the first half.

In the South, the Hyosung Vina plant offered its PP coating at 41,000 VND/kg incl. VAT early this week, but buyers are still bidding considerably below this level. Some sellers cut the offer for Lotte's PP coating M9600 to 44,000 VND/kg incl. VAT — a sharp drop of up to 3,000 VND/kg versus last week — but buyers are still not ready to sign for import.
For PP copo, the cheapest currently on offer is Hanwha Korea-origin PP, quoted at 33,000–34,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

summary: Qamar offered LLDPE Film at USD 980-990/ton CIF HCMC and HDPE Film F1 at USD 1,040/ton CIF
Sibur cut PE sharply at Hai Phong: LLDPE VND 30,400/kg and HDPE Film VND 32,000/kg
Domestic LDPE fell to VND 39,000-41,000/kg; LLDPE and HDPE remain under strong downward pressure
paras: Today, June 30, Qamar (Saudi Arabia) offered PE import prices for a shipment expected to load in August, with an arrival time of about 1.5–2 months (i.e. arriving in Vietnam around late September/early October 2026). Accordingly, Qamar's LLDPE Film FD21HS/FD21HN/CD18N… offer is at 980–990 USD/ton CIF HCMC, and the HDPE Film F1 offer is at 1,040 USD/ton CIF.

This is Qamar's first round of offers since the Strait of Hormuz reopened in late June, against a backdrop in which the Middle East shipping market is still not fully stable. The supplier said that traffic through Hormuz cannot yet be confirmed to have returned to normal, while major shipping lines are still mainly operating routes via Jeddah, keeping freight costs significantly higher than before the Iran war broke out.

Also today, the distributor of Lotte Chemical (South Korea) announced new firm selling prices for several PP and PE shipments for August 2026 delivery to buyers in Vietnam. The firm selling price for LLDPE film is 1,000 USD/ton CIF; for large-volume orders above 800 tons, the price is deeply discounted to around 960 USD/ton; LLDPE injection UL814 is at 1,030 USD/ton CIF — down by as much as 130 USD/ton versus two weeks ago; HDPE yarn/injection is offered at 1,070–1,080 USD/ton CIF…

The distributor of Sibur (Russia) today simultaneously cut its offers on many PE products delivered at Hai Phong port, quoted in VND. Accordingly, LLDPE film prices fell sharply by 1,600 VND/kg versus last week, down to 30,400 VND/kg incl. VAT; the mLLDPE 30183FE offer dropped 1,500 VND/kg to 32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; HDPE film grade offers fell 1,100 VND/kg to 32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; HDPE blow offers fell 1,400 VND/kg to 33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT… Sibur is also offering many near/off grades at cheap prices.
Domestically, after cautious adjustments early in the week, today many large commercial sellers in both the South and the North have cut PE prices very sharply.

For LDPE Film, the offer level has fallen deeply to 39,000–41,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, i.e. down 1,000–4,000 VND/kg versus last week (depending on product, origin and region). For example, in the South some US- and Korea-origin LDPE Film products have been offered down to 39,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; even PTT Thailand's LDPE 2426K has been offered down to 40,000–40,500 VND/kg incl. VAT. In the North, offers for Middle East-origin LDPE Film such as Sabic's 4024WN, Tasnee's 4025AS/1925AS or Borouge's FT6230 are also only fluctuating around 40,000–41,000 VND/kg incl. VAT…

For LLDPE, in the North some traders have cut offers for Sabic's LLDPE Film 218WJ and Qamar's FD21HS down to 31,500–32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT depending on volume, a sharp drop of 1,000–1,500 VND/kg versus the end of last week. Most China-origin LLDPE Film has been offered below 31,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, competing strongly with Sibur Russia's goods.

For HDPE, although the price level has also fallen quickly over the past 10 days, it has basically held at a fairly high level. Today, Qamar's HDPE Film F1 was quoted around 36,000 VND/kg incl. VAT. Even so, buyers are pushing prices down hard and believe prices will fall further within a few days, after Qamar just announced an import offer of 1,040 USD/ton CIF. Offers for HDPE yarn/injection/blow products are commonly 33,000–36,000 VND/kg incl. VAT…

summary: China PVC oversupply intensified; operating rate near 70% and social inventory above 1.27 million tons
Imported oil-based PVC into Vietnam is commonly at USD 670-700/ton CIF
Domestic SG-5 is at VND 20,500/kg, AGC PM66R at VND 20,900/kg, and Shintech at VND 21,500/kg
paras: In China the domestic PVC market continued to weaken sharply in the closing days of June as oversupply pressure mounted and consumption sat at a seasonal low. Spot prices fell across both main production routes — calcium-carbide-based (coal-based) and ethylene-based (oil-based). Notably, the entire PVC production chain has now slipped into the red, with an average loss of about 301 RMB/ton for calcium-carbide technology and 468 RMB/ton for ethylene technology, showing that price competition is becoming increasingly fierce.

Supply pressure continued to build as most plants completed maintenance and restarted in unison. As of mid-June, the industry-wide operating rate had climbed to nearly 70%, with calcium-carbide-based capacity holding high above 77% and ethylene-based capacity recovering quickly on lower feedstock costs. Social inventory continued to set a record high at more than 1.27 million tons, more than double the same period last year. The heavy inventory burden is forcing traders to ramp up sales at low prices to clear stock.

Even so, China's PVC offers into Vietnam are not especially attractive versus goods of other origins, mainly because of fierce competitive pressure as many large producers in Southeast Asia and the US have cut their offers very deeply. The prevailing transaction level for imported PVC (oil-based) into Vietnam is currently 670–700 USD/ton CIF. Buyers are holding fairly healthy inventories and only topping up when necessary.

In domestic trade, China-origin coal-based PVC SG-5 remains the lowest at 20,500 VND/kg incl. VAT in the North. Although it is the cheapest in the market, the gap to oil-based goods has narrowed to only about 500–1,000 VND/kg, so it is not attractive to buyers either.

In the South in particular, oil-based PVC PM66R from domestic producer AGC Vina is the lowest at 20,900 VND/kg incl. VAT, exerting very strong competitive pressure. Shintech US-origin PVC SE950/SE1000 has been offered by a large distributor at 21,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, significantly below the general price level of last week…

“Many domestic distributors and commercial PVC sellers are starting to feel the losses. For instance, some buyers shared that a US-origin PVC shipment they contracted to import at 790–800 USD/ton CIF (before 5% import duty) has just landed, while the new offer for Shintech's PVC has now fallen to 670 USD/ton CIF — so the freshly arrived goods are already carrying a loss. There is word that one leading distributor holds up to several hundred tons of oil-based PVC contracted at a cost of around 28,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, while the current transaction level has dropped to 22,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.
Even some buyers who contracted Thailand- and Indonesia-origin PVC with fast delivery have still not cleared in time the inventory contracted during the high-price period”.

regional.l: Lotte LLDPE Film CIF - Large Order

editorial.changedNote: Jun 30 saw another broad-based downshift: PE cuts deepened, HD blow recorded the largest daily decline, while PP and PVC remained pressured by Korean and China-origin competition.
editorial.regionalNote: Regional references limited to USD/ton import/reference cards with explicit decrease/discount indications.
